pi_items.metadata_value: John Lewis Voting Rights Advancement Act of 2021
pi_items.verified_value: John R. Lewis Voting Rights Advancement Act of 2021
pi_items.source: Congress.gov official bill page and multiple official sources including Rock the Vote, Brennan Center for Justice, and Congressional Records

pi_items.verified_value: Sewell (primary sponsor: Terri A. Sewell, D-AL-7); Jackson Lee was a cosponsor only, and her actual last name is 'Lee', not 'Jackson Lee'
pi_items.source: Congress.gov H.R. 4 page and Rep. Sewell's official press release: https://sewell.house.gov/2021/8/rep-sewell-introduces-hr-4-john-r-lewis-voting-rights-advancement-act
Rep. Sewell's press release and multiple Congressional sources confirming introduction on August 17, 2021

privileged_info_summary: H.R.4 is a real legislative bill from the 117th Congress (2021) with the official title 'John R. Lewis Voting Rights Advancement Act of 2021' (includes middle initial 'R.'). The bill was introduced on August 17, 2021 and passed the U.S. House of Representatives on August 24, 2021. The primary sponsor is Rep. Terri A. Sewell (D-AL-7), not Sheila Jackson Lee. Sheila Jackson Lee was an original cosponsor. The bill was presented in the U.S. House of Representatives during the 117th Congress.
